actionUnits.condition: Validate neighbor-driven ignition logic for Fire MonoBehaviour (script guid: 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d, component fileID: 2592690533154406537). Scene config confirms this component matches the provided Fire script: isOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, onNeighborsThreshold=2, maxNeighborDistance=4, minNeighborDownDistance=3, downMultiplier=2, totalHitTime=5, maxHitInterval=2, neighbors=[], mainEffectGo fileID=1849464941111748095, BoxCollider enabled (no Rigidbody). To trigger automatic turn-on, at least 2 neighbor Fire components within 4 units must be 'isOn'; if any neighbor is 3+ units below, ignition speeds up by downMultiplier. Expected behavior when condition is met: shouldTurnOn becomes true, after timeToOn elapses isOn becomes true, UpdateFireState(true) plays particles and notifies neighbors, FireController.Instance.FireChanged(+1) is invoked.

Extinguish via IWaterInteractable.WaterHit. Requires the fire to already be isOn=true (set by neighbor ignition above or externally). Simulate repeated water impacts so that each hit occurs within maxHitInterval (2s) and cumulative currentHitTime reaches totalHitTime (5s). Expected behavior: while isTurningOff && isOn, currentHitTime accumulates; if any interval exceeds maxHitInterval, the extinguish process resets; upon reaching totalHitTime, isOn=false, UpdateFireState(true) stops particles and notifies neighbors, FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1). Note: Fire.WaterHit(Vector3) requires a parameter and is invoked by the water system on collision; manual parameterless Trigger is not available in this plan.
